Section Title: 2025 review, 2026 preview > ... > Figure 1: Rare earth supply > Source: Wood Mackenzie
Content:
On a practical level, the Trump administration is helping to reinvigorate interest in the mining sector, with moves to ease and expedite project permitting, while making government funding available to project developers through grants and loans, which lower their cost of capital and enhance their chances of economic success. The US government has gone further than this in some instances, including taking direct equity stakes in some companies, participation at the project level, and providing floor pricing for mineral commodities it particularly wants.
The US government is also reinvigorating the nuclear power sector, including through an $80B agreement to build new generation capacity, which will benefit the uranium sector in the US and Canada. Expect more of the same in 2026. Here is Rick on uranium:

Section Title: Critical Minerals: Energy > Rule Investment Newsletter #9 > Uranium
Content:
Around 150Mlb of yellowcake was produced in 2024, but 190Mlb was consumed, which is eating into inventories. The US Energy Information Administration (EIA) warns of a uranium shortfall over the next 10 years as mine supply fails to keep pace with expected increases in demand. It sees a 50Mlb deficit in 2025 growing. The *Uranium Market Outlook* projected the production gap at ~51Mlb through 2026, ~355Mlb through 2035, 890Mlb through 2040, and 1.75Blb through 2045. Uranium prices have risen to about $80/lb on the back of shortfalls in production from Cameco in Canada, and Kazatomprom in Kazakhstan, and pricing is still not high enough to stimulate new capacity construction. US utilities are the world’s largest consumers of uranium, with a current demand of 47Mlb/y. There are 439 nuclear power plants in operation globally with 70 under construction and 108 planned. Builds are at a 25-year high. Here are Rick’s thoughts:
 ... 
Section Title: Companies > Arizona Sonoran Copper (TSX:ASCU/OTC:ASCUF) > Cameco (NYSE:CCJ/TSX:CCO)
Content:
Share price change YoY 75%
Rick believes the surest way to make sure money in uranium is to buy Cameco. Cameco produced 10.6Mlb of uranium in the first six months of 2025. In October, together with Westinghouse Electric and Brookfield Asset Management, it announced that the US government entered into a strategic partnership to accelerate the deployment of nuclear power that will include at least $80B of new reactors to be built across the US. Brookfield and Cameco formed a strategic partnership in October 2022 to acquire Westinghouse. Rick says:
Cameco may fall 35%, but it’s the bellwether for uranium, and uranium is a 15-year no-brainer.
